item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My theory?
+ show block
– hide block

The caretaker becomes the scp at the end of each cycle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What.

The fuck?

I like it. Sure wish I knew what the hell was going on though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Leans a bit too hard on the hidden data angle imo, but still becomes a very interesting mystery with a novel concept. +1

item.title: Junior Staff - Site Crit
item.content:encoded:
Crit/Opinion
Close

So I don't have very much in the form of negative things to say about this. There are some spots where I feel the wording or phrasing could have been different, but for the most part, these things fall under personal opinion (Example: Use of words like annihilates, while its descriptive doesn't ring as clinical in my opinion). Aside from small things like that I feel interviews A and B feel a tad stiff, especially compared to interview C which is a lot more fluid. That said, the anomaly is interesting, the piece is refreshingly brief (of course), and very well written. It is an easy +1 from me.



Site crit : Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to DrAkimoto or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh, and 5k's effect is cool as hell, even if it makes for one hell of a sad story. teleohazard? cool as hell concept, and not one I believe I've seen here before. neato. it amazes me how even with thousands of articles, people still find a way to create novel stories and concepts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
whoa.

being one of the dumber readers here, I'm not sure what the implication of that last line is although i have some ideas that I'm too embarrassed to share. :-) but I really liked this. it's *very* brief compared to almost every other 5k entry I've read so far — which earns it points automatically for me at this point, since a couple of those long entries just couldn't hold my attention and it took me the better part of a day to read 2 of them.

but it's also FASCINATING. you totally nailed the theme, in my not at all humble opinion. this piece made me feel uncomfortable but also terribly curious — I want to know more. I want to read more, despite what I just said in the first paragraph. poor 5k and poor researchers. :-/ +1
